---
title: "TensorToFeatureVector"
---

## Overview

`TensorToFeatureVector` decodes a global feature descriptor from a model output
tensor into a flat `std::vector<float>`. `SlotAssembler` writes the result to
`TensorData` in DataManager (one row per frame in batch inference).

**Header:** `src/DeepLearning/channel_decoding/TensorToFeatureVector.hpp`  
**Registry:** `DecoderFactory::create("TensorToFeatureVector")` — `outputTypeName()` is `"std::vector<float>"`

Dispatched via [`DecoderDispatch`](DecoderDispatch.qmd) when
`OutputBindingData::decoder` holds `FeatureVectorDecoderParams`.

## Parameters

```cpp
struct FeatureVectorDecoderParams {};  // no user-configurable fields
```

## Tensor layout

The decoder accepts **1D** or **2D** tensors:

| Rank | Layout | Behavior |
|------|--------|----------|
| 1    | `[C]`  | Returns all `C` feature values. `ctx.batch_index` is ignored. |
| 2    | `[B, C]` | Returns the `C`-length vector at `ctx.batch_index`. |

`DecoderContext::source_channel`, `height`, `width`, and `target_image_size` are
not used by this decoder.

This decoder is typically used after a post-encoder such as `GlobalAvgPoolModule`,
which reduces a spatial `[B, C, H, W]` feature map to `[B, C]`.

The tensor is converted to CPU `float32` and copied with `memcpy` into the result
vector.

## Validation

`decode()` validates at runtime:

- Tensor is defined and at least 1-dimensional
- Tensor rank is 1 or 2 (not 0D, 3D, or higher)
- For 2D inputs, `batch_index` is in `[0, B)`

Invalid inputs throw `std::invalid_argument` or `std::out_of_range` after logging
at `spdlog::debug` level.

## Batch inference

For batch runs with this decoder, `SlotAssembler` accumulates per-frame feature
rows and writes a single ordinal 2D `TensorData` matrix at batch completion.
See [post-encoder modules](../post_encoder_modules.qmd).

## Tests

`tests/DeepLearning/channel_decoding/TensorToFeatureVector.test.cpp` — unbatched
1D decode, batched 2D decode, rank validation.

Filter with `ctest -R "TensorToFeatureVector"`.
